proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving `treatment_trastuzumab` have higher mean `pfs_months` than patients not receiving `treatment_trastuzumab`, after adjustment for clinical covariates and other treatments.
Patients receiving `treatment_palbociclib` have higher mean `pfs_months` than patients not receiving `treatment_palbociclib`, after adjustment for clinical covariates and other treatments.
Patients receiving `treatment_pembrolizumab` have higher mean `pfs_months` than patients not receiving `treatment_pembrolizumab`, after adjustment for clinical covariates and other treatments.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ treatments + demographics + disease burden + biomarkers + inflammatory markers
analyses.result_summary: In a multivariable OLS model adjusted for demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, inflammatory labs, and other treatments, `treatment_trastuzumab` was associated with -0.009 months shorter mean `pfs_months` (z=-0.75, p=0.4548).

In a multivariable OLS model adjusted for demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, inflammatory labs, and other treatments, `treatment_palbociclib` was associated with 1.102 months longer mean `pfs_months` (z=103.14, p=<1e-300).

In a multivariable OLS model adjusted for demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, inflammatory labs, and other treatments, `treatment_pembrolizumab` was associated with -0.02 months shorter mean `pfs_months` (z=-1.38, p=0.1674).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `treatment_trastuzumab` with `pfs_months` is larger in `her2_positive` patients than in `her2_positive`-negative patients.

analyses.code: OLS with treatment_trastuzumab * her2_positive interaction and clinical covariate adjustment
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted trastuzumab-by-HER2-positive interaction: the interaction estimate was 0.022 months (z=0.68, p=0.4948).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `treatment_trastuzumab` with `pfs_months` is larger in patients with `her2_amplification` than in patients without `her2_amplification`.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted trastuzumab-by-HER2-amplification interaction: the interaction estimate was -0.052 months (z=-0.69, p=0.4882).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `treatment_trastuzumab` with `pfs_months` is weaker in patients with `her2_low` than in patients with `her2_low` equal to 0.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted trastuzumab-by-HER2-low interaction: the interaction estimate was -0.012 months (z=-0.45, p=0.6540).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `treatment_palbociclib` with `pfs_months` is larger in `er_positive` patients than in `er_positive`-negative patients.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted palbociclib-by-ER-positive interaction: the interaction estimate was 1.559 months (z=70.16, p=<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `treatment_palbociclib` with `pfs_months` is larger in `pr_positive` patients than in `pr_positive`-negative patients.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted palbociclib-by-PR-positive interaction: the interaction estimate was 1.028 months (z=47.25, p=<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `treatment_tamoxifen` with `pfs_months` is larger in `er_positive` patients than in `er_positive`-negative patients.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted tamoxifen-by-ER-positive interaction: the interaction estimate was 0.03 months (z=1.26, p=0.2092).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `treatment_olaparib` with `pfs_months` is larger in patients with `brca1_mutation` than in patients without `brca1_mutation`.
The positive association of `treatment_olaparib` with `pfs_months` is larger in patients with `brca2_mutation` than in patients without `brca2_mutation`.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted olaparib-by-BRCA1 interaction: the interaction estimate was 0.078 months (z=0.71, p=0.4789).
Adjusted olaparib-by-BRCA2 interaction: the interaction estimate was -0.045 months (z=-0.4, p=0.6874).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients not receiving it after adjustment for covariates, reflecting use in more aggressive disease or treatment resistance.

analyses.result_summary: In a multivariable OLS model adjusted for demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, inflammatory labs, and other treatments, `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` was associated with 0.033 months longer mean `pfs_months` (z=1.96, p=0.0501).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The association of `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` with `pfs_months` is more negative among `stage_iv` patients than among non-`stage_iv` patients.
The association of `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` with `pfs_months` is more negative among patients with `liver_mets` than among patients without `liver_mets`.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted sacituzumab-by-stage-IV interaction: the interaction estimate was 0.034 months (z=0.91, p=0.3623).
Adjusted sacituzumab-by-liver-mets interaction: the interaction estimate was 0.014 months (z=0.29, p=0.7696).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `stage_iv` have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients without `stage_iv`, after adjustment for treatment and other clinical covariates.
Patients with `liver_mets` have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients without `liver_mets`, after adjustment for treatment and other clinical covariates.

analyses.result_summary: In a multivariable OLS model adjusted for demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, inflammatory labs, and other treatments, `stage_iv` was associated with -1.549 months shorter mean `pfs_months` (z=-139.38, p=<1e-300).

In a multivariable OLS model adjusted for demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, inflammatory labs, and other treatments, `liver_mets` was associated with -0.019 months shorter mean `pfs_months` (z=-1.31, p=0.1919).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients who are both `stage_iv` and have `liver_mets` have lower `pfs_months` than expected from the additive effects of `stage_iv` and `liver_mets` alone.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted stage-IV-by-liver-mets interaction: the interaction estimate was 0.044 months (z=1.44, p=0.1510).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `has_brain_mets` have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients without `has_brain_mets`, after adjustment for treatment and other clinical covariates.
Patients with `bone_mets` have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients without `bone_mets`, after adjustment for treatment and other clinical covariates.

analyses.result_summary: In a multivariable OLS model adjusted for demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, inflammatory labs, and other treatments, `has_brain_mets` was associated with -0.983 months shorter mean `pfs_months` (z=-58.42, p=<1e-300).

In a multivariable OLS model adjusted for demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, inflammatory labs, and other treatments, `bone_mets` was associated with 0.014 months longer mean `pfs_months` (z=1.09, p=0.2749).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients who have both `has_brain_mets` and `pleural_effusion` have lower `pfs_months` than expected from the additive effects of those two variables alone.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted brain-mets-by-pleural-effusion interaction: the interaction estimate was -0.061 months (z=-1.29, p=0.1954).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `albumin_g_dl` is associated with higher mean `pfs_months`, after adjustment for disease burden, biomarkers, and treatments.
Higher `ldh_u_l` is associated with lower mean `pfs_months`, after adjustment for disease burden, biomarkers, and treatments.

analyses.result_summary: A one-SD higher `albumin_g_dl` was associated with 0.239 months higher mean `pfs_months` (z=46.87, p=<1e-300).
A one-SD higher `ldh_u_l` was associated with -0.042 months change in mean `pfs_months` (z=-8.23, p=2.22e-16).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `crp_mg_l` is associated with lower mean `pfs_months`, after adjustment for disease burden, biomarkers, and treatments.
Higher `nlr` is associated with lower mean `pfs_months`, after adjustment for disease burden, biomarkers, and treatments.

analyses.result_summary: A one-SD higher `crp_mg_l` was associated with -0.001 months change in mean `pfs_months` (z=-0.21, p=0.8373).
A one-SD higher `nlr` was associated with -0.003 months change in mean `pfs_months` (z=-0.61, p=0.5416).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `ki67_pct` is associated with lower mean `pfs_months`, after adjustment for stage, biomarkers, disease burden, and treatments.
Larger `tumor_size_cm` is associated with lower mean `pfs_months`, after adjustment for stage, biomarkers, disease burden, and treatments.

analyses.result_summary: A one-SD higher `ki67_pct` was associated with -0.23 months change in mean `pfs_months` (z=-45.15, p=<1e-300).
A one-SD larger `tumor_size_cm` was associated with 0.002 months change in mean `pfs_months` (z=0.45, p=0.6529).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `weight_loss_pct_6mo` is associated with lower mean `pfs_months`, after adjustment for disease burden, biomarkers, and treatments.
Higher `ecog_ps` is associated with lower mean `pfs_months`, after adjustment for disease burden, biomarkers, and treatments.

analyses.result_summary: A one-SD higher `weight_loss_pct_6mo` was associated with -0.296 months change in mean `pfs_months` (z=-58.22, p=<1e-300).

In a multivariable OLS model adjusted for demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, inflammatory labs, and other treatments, `ecog_ps` was associated with -1.172 months shorter mean `pfs_months` (z=-156.04, p=<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: `er_positive` patients have higher mean `pfs_months` than `er_positive`-negative patients after adjustment for treatments and other covariates.
`pr_positive` patients have higher mean `pfs_months` than `pr_positive`-negative patients after adjustment for treatments and other covariates.

analyses.result_summary: In a multivariable OLS model adjusted for demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, inflammatory labs, and other treatments, `er_positive` was associated with 0.532 months longer mean `pfs_months` (z=35.65, p=<1e-300).

In a multivariable OLS model adjusted for demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, inflammatory labs, and other treatments, `pr_positive` was associated with 0.014 months longer mean `pfs_months` (z=0.99, p=0.3211).

proposed_hypotheses.text: `er_positive` and `pr_positive` together are associated with higher `pfs_months` than expected from the additive effects of `er_positive` and `pr_positive` alone.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted ER-positive-by-PR-positive interaction: the interaction estimate was 0.023 months (z=0.73, p=0.4673).

proposed_hypotheses.text: `her2_positive` patients have higher mean `pfs_months` than `her2_positive`-negative patients after adjustment for HER2-directed therapy and other covariates.
`her2_low` patients have higher mean `pfs_months` than patients with `her2_low` equal to 0 after adjustment for treatments and other covariates.

analyses.result_summary: In a multivariable OLS model adjusted for demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, inflammatory labs, and other treatments, `her2_positive` was associated with -0.471 months shorter mean `pfs_months` (z=-33.23, p=<1e-300).

In a multivariable OLS model adjusted for demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, inflammatory labs, and other treatments, `her2_low` was associated with 0.002 months longer mean `pfs_months` (z=0.17, p=0.8642).

proposed_hypotheses.text: `her2_positive` patients have higher adjusted mean `pfs_months` than `her2_low` patients, comparing the two HER2-defined subgroups after adjustment for treatments and other covariates.

analyses.result_summary: The adjusted contrast for `her2_positive` minus `her2_low` was -0.473 months (z=-32.4, p=<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: `pik3ca_mutation` patients have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients without `pik3ca_mutation`, after adjustment for treatments and other covariates.
`brca1_mutation` patients have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients without `brca1_mutation`, after adjustment for treatments and other covariates.
`brca2_mutation` patients have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients without `brca2_mutation`, after adjustment for treatments and other covariates.

analyses.result_summary: In a multivariable OLS model adjusted for demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, inflammatory labs, and other treatments, `pik3ca_mutation` was associated with -0.582 months shorter mean `pfs_months` (z=-54.41, p=<1e-300).

In a multivariable OLS model adjusted for demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, inflammatory labs, and other treatments, `brca1_mutation` was associated with -0.068 months shorter mean `pfs_months` (z=-2.12, p=0.0340).

In a multivariable OLS model adjusted for demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, inflammatory labs, and other treatments, `brca2_mutation` was associated with -0.042 months shorter mean `pfs_months` (z=-1.29, p=0.1985).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `treatment_pembrolizumab` with `pfs_months` is smaller in patients with `autoimmune_disease` than in patients without `autoimmune_disease`.
The positive association of `treatment_pembrolizumab` with `pfs_months` decreases as `nlr` increases.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted pembrolizumab-by-autoimmune-disease interaction: the interaction estimate was 0.021 months (z=0.32, p=0.7464).
Adjusted pembrolizumab-by-NLR-z interaction: the per-SD-NLR interaction estimate was -0.014 months (z=-0.97, p=0.3316).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Routine comorbidity indicators (`diabetes_mellitus`, `hypertension`, `copd`, `chronic_kidney_disease`, and `heart_failure`) have smaller associations with `pfs_months` than tumor burden and treatment-biomarker interactions after adjustment.
Higher `age_years` is associated with higher mean `pfs_months`, after adjustment for disease burden, biomarkers, treatments, and inflammatory markers.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted comorbidity coefficients were small relative to major tumor/treatment effects: diabetes_mellitus: 0.002 months, p=0.8672; hypertension: 0 months, p=0.9742; copd: -0.001 months, p=0.9073; chronic_kidney_disease: -0.013 months, p=0.4211; heart_failure: 0.007 months, p=0.6629.

In a multivariable OLS model adjusted for demographics, disease burden, biomarkers, inflammatory labs, and other treatments, `age_years` was associated with 0.176 months longer mean `pfs_months` (z=343.56, p=<1e-300).
